title: The Brutalist
logline: A Hungarian-Jewish architect survives the Holocaust and emigrates to America, where he struggles to rebuild his life and career until a wealthy industrialist commissions him to design a monumental community center—a project that becomes both his masterpiece and his undoing.
antagonistOrConflict: Harrison Lee Van Buren Sr. and the corrupting influence of American capitalism on artistic integrity
themes: The brutality of the American Dream

actBreaks.description: Van Buren commissions László to design the Institute, transforming him from laborer to architect and launching the central project that will define the rest of the narrative.

validation.ruling: Scene #60 functions as a major turning point where Van Buren, drunk and vulnerable, shares his intimate family history with László in the foyer during the Christmas party, fundamentally shifting their relationship from employer-employee to something more complex and personal—the story cannot return to its previous professional distance.

actBreaks.description: László destroys his model in fury after Van Buren's financial manipulations threaten the project, marking the collapse of their partnership and László's descent.

validation.ruling: Scene #110 is the irreversible act break where László smashes his model in fury after being fired, declaring 'it's over' while Erzsébet watches—this marks the complete collapse of the Van Buren Center project and forces the narrative into its final act of consequences and confrontation.

naturalStructure.structuralPersonality: This is a three-act epic with an overture, intermission, and epilogue—structured like an opera. Part One (scenes 1-60) chronicles László's arrival and survival in America, culminating in the commission. Part Two (scenes 61-110) follows the construction of the Institute and the reunion with his wife Erzsébet, ending with the project's financial collapse. Part Three (scenes 111-164) depicts László's decline, Erzsébet's confrontation with Van Buren, and the posthumous recognition at the Venice Biennale. The script employs a novelistic patience, allowing scenes to breathe and accumulate emotional weight rather than rushing toward plot points. The intermission at page 69 literally divides the film, reinforcing its operatic ambitions.
naturalStructure.pacingNotes: The pacing is deliberately slow and accumulative in Part One, with many brief scenes (average 1.3 pages) creating a mosaic of László's immigrant experience. Part Two expands into longer, more sustained sequences as the Institute takes shape. The final act accelerates emotionally while maintaining formal control, culminating in the devastating single-take confrontation (scenes 145-155) and the elegiac epilogue. Key tonal shifts occur at scene 52 (the jazz bar overdose), scene 80 (Erzsébet's arrival), and scene 121 (Van Buren's monologue in Italy).

incitingIncident.description: Harry Lee shows László and Attila his father's dark octagonal study filled with books and asks for shelves to the ceiling and reading lamps, then tells them to estimate whatever they need.
incitingIncident.sceneHeading: INT. VAN BUREN’S STUDY - MOMENTS LATER

validation.ruling: Scene #30 establishes the central conflict when Harry Lee hires László to renovate Van Buren's study with a budget of $600-700, launching László's professional relationship with the Van Buren family that drives the entire narrative forward.

midpoint.description: László presents his finished model to Van Buren, Harry Lee, Maggie Lee, and Leslie Woodrow, explaining the 648 square meter design with convertible multi-functional spaces and a spherical chapel at its heart, while Van Buren gets a haircut and praises the work.

validation.ruling: Scene #80 is merely the arrival of László's wife and niece, not a transformative moment. Scene #74 (p60, 45.8%) is the true midpoint where Van Buren reveals he shared László's plans with another architect and passes him a file with concerns—this is the point of no return that shifts László from reactive craftsman to proactive architect fighting for his vision, raising the personal and professional stakes significantly.

climax.description: Erzsébet enters the dining room where Van Buren, Harry Lee, and associates are seated; she refuses to sit and tells them she has something very difficult to say, then accuses Van Buren of raping her husband in Italy.
climax.sceneHeading: INT. VAN BUREN ESTATE - DINING ROOM - CONTINUOUS

validation.ruling: Scene #145 is merely Erzsébet's arrival in the foyer, not the confrontation itself. Scene #146 (p123, 94%) is the true climax where Erzsébet enters the dining room and directly accuses Van Buren of raping her husband in Italy—this is the peak dramatic confrontation that resolves the central power dynamic and completes the character arc.

scenes.heading: INT. INTERVIEW ROOM IN VAS COUNTY - MORNING LIGHT
scenes.summary: A border officer interrogates Zsófia, a traumatized young woman, in Hungarian and English, asking about her family and identity while she remains silent.

scenes.heading: INT. SHIP - LOWER DECK - DUSK
scenes.summary: Immigrants sleep in bunks on a ship's lower deck while Erzsébet's voiceover letter reveals Zsófia is with her but ill and under suspicion from officials.

scenes.heading: INT. SHIP - STAIRWELL - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: László and another man squeeze through a narrow doorway and climb three flights of stairs as Erzsébet's voiceover continues describing their situation.

scenes.heading: EXT. SHIP DECK - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: László and his companion reach the upper deck among dozens of immigrants and see the Statue of Liberty as Erzsébet's letter urges László to go to America.

scenes.heading: INT. HIAS CENTER BASEMENT - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Two HIAS representatives address a crowded room of Jewish émigrés wearing numbered cards, speaking in English and Yiddish simultaneously.

scenes.heading: EXT. NYC EAST RIVER DOCKS - NIGHT
scenes.summary: A foghorn blows as the camera pans across prostitutes talking amongst themselves near the NYC East River docks at night.

scenes.heading: INT. BROTHEL - LATER
scenes.summary: László remains unable to become aroused while a prostitute attempts to stimulate him, as his Hungarian refugee friend has sex in the background and shouts at László.

scenes.heading: INT. BROTHEL HALLWAY - LATER
scenes.summary: The madame offers László boys instead and invites him to stay for a free movie screening with champagne in the basement.

scenes.heading: INT. BROTHEL BASEMENT - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: László enters a makeshift cinema in the brothel basement holding champagne while silent pornography from the 1930s plays on a projector.

scenes.heading: EXT. CHINATOWN - DAWN
scenes.summary: László and the Hungarian refugee run drunkenly to catch a departing bus at dawn, embrace like brothers, and László boards while his friend stays behind.

scenes.heading: EXT. BUS - DAY
scenes.summary: The bus travels through Philadelphia past various architectural landmarks including City Hall, the Art Museum, and Pennsylvania Station.

scenes.heading: INT. BUS - LATER
scenes.summary: László rests his head against the frosted bus window and sees his cousin Attila waiting among onlookers in heavy snow.

scenes.heading: EXT. BUS / DOWNTOWN PHILADELPHIA - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Attila embraces László and tells him in Hungarian that Erzsébet is alive and with Zsófia, causing László to buckle and wail emotionally.
scenes.turningPointNote: László learns his wife and niece survived, transforming his emotional state and giving him hope and purpose in America.

scenes.heading: INT. FURNITURE SHOWROOM - LATER
scenes.summary: Attila and Audrey show László around their furniture showroom, explaining their custom cabinetry business and why they left Manhattan for Philadelphia.

scenes.heading: INT. FURNITURE SHOWROOM - BACKROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: László reads Erzsébet's letter confirming she is alive, weeps while stroking the text lovingly, then begins sketching an architectural drawing.

scenes.heading: EXT. OLD CITY CHURCH SOUP KITCHEN - MORNING
scenes.summary: László stands alone in a soup kitchen line among impoverished families while a uniformed man named Gordon plays I Spy with his son William, who stares at László.

scenes.heading: INT. ABOVE GROUND TROLLEY - LATER
scenes.summary: László stands in a packed trolley car, watches a woman's purse, then repositions himself and successfully pickpockets one of two businessmen.

scenes.heading: INT. THE CONGREGATION MIKVEH ISRAEL - LATER
scenes.summary: László sits wearing a kippah during a service at Congregation Mikveh Israel and recites Hebrew prayers with the congregation.

scenes.heading: INT. THE CONGREGATION MIKVEH ISRAEL - LATER
scenes.summary: László discreetly tells Rabbi Zunz that his wife and niece are stuck at the Austrian boundary, and the rabbi agrees to speak with him after the service.

scenes.heading: INT./ EXT. WORKSHOP/ FOUNDRY - DAY
scenes.summary: László meticulously constructs a chaise lounge and two tubular Bauhaus-style chairs, directing welding work and measuring leather with precision.

scenes.heading: EXT. FURNITURE SHOWROOM - LATER
scenes.summary: Audrey examines László's finished chairs and shelving unit in the shop window, uncertain how to display them, and tells László they look like tricycles.

scenes.heading: EXT. EMPLOYEE RESTROOM - MORNING
scenes.summary: László shaves in the employee restroom while practicing an American accent by reciting the Peter Piper tongue twister with emphasized R sounds.

scenes.heading: EXT. FURNITURE SHOWROOM - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Attila grabs László outside the bathroom and urgently tells him an important customer is inside who wants built-in work at a residence.

scenes.heading: INT. FURNITURE SHOWROOM - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Harry Lee Van Buren introduces himself to László, apologizes for interrupting his shave, and explains he followed Attila's chairs from the shop window.

scenes.heading: EXT. COUNTY ROADS - LATER
scenes.summary: The camera shows asphalt rushing forward as the road bends and curves from a low angle.

scenes.heading: EXT. ATTILA’S BEDFORD VAN - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Attila drives behind Harry Lee's speeding sports car and tells László the Van Burens own nine hundred acres and that the father got rich adapting production techniques.

scenes.heading: EXT. VAN BUREN GATES - LATER
scenes.summary: Harry Lee opens the estate gates, shouts directions to park near the main house, and Attila drives his Bedford van through.

scenes.heading: EXT. VAN BUREN ESTATE - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: The camera shows the striking Van Buren estate framed by a tree-lined driveway as ominous music plays.

scenes.heading: INT. VAN BUREN ESTATE - FOYER - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Harry Lee leads László and Attila through the foyer past modernist sculptures while Attila tells Harry that László is a licensed architect specializing in renovations.

scenes.heading: INT. VAN BUREN’S STUDY - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Harry Lee shows László and Attila his father's dark octagonal study filled with books and asks for shelves to the ceiling and reading lamps, then tells them to estimate whatever they need.
scenes.turningPointNote: László receives his first significant architectural commission in America, opening the door to his professional reemergence and entanglement with the Van Buren family.

scenes.heading: INT. ATTILA’S APARTMENT - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Attila drunkenly dances with Audrey to Dinah Shore while László watches from the kitchen table, then Attila praises László for negotiating a high price with Van Buren.

scenes.heading: INT. ATTILA’S APARTMENT - LATER
scenes.summary: László drunkenly urinates in the bathtub while Audrey watches and smokes, then she asks when his wife will join him and notes there's no room in the storage space.

scenes.heading: INT. VAN BUREN’S STUDY - DAY
scenes.summary: Attila and László remove the existing Art Deco shelving units from Van Buren's study.

scenes.heading: EXT. VAN BUREN ESTATE - DUSK
scenes.summary: Attila and László craft new shelving units next to a pile of debris outside the Van Buren estate at dusk.

scenes.heading: INT. VAN BUREN’S STUDY - EVENING
scenes.summary: László sweeps the empty, stripped study alone in the evening, then stops at the center and observes the space.

scenes.heading: INT. VAN BUREN’S STUDY - NEW DAY
scenes.summary: László directs hired men including Gordon to simultaneously lift large plywood plates that enclose the octagonal room, sealing the windows in darkness except for light from the dome above.

scenes.heading: INT. VAN BUREN’S STUDY - NEW DAY
scenes.summary: László instructs Gordon and Attila to set each panel at 45 degrees so the bookshelves fan outward, with varying heights to accommodate larger volumes.

scenes.heading: EXT. VAN BUREN ESTATE ROOF - DAY
scenes.summary: László directs the crew on the roof as they use a pulley system to lift the glass dome, but one man swings the crane arm too fast, nearly causing an accident.

scenes.heading: INT. VAN BUREN’S STUDY - DAY
scenes.summary: The crew replaces the stained-glass dome with clear glass, flooding the room with light, and László pushes his chaise lounge into the center of the illuminated space.

scenes.heading: INT. VAN BUREN’S STUDY - DUSK
scenes.summary: László and Attila remove protective linens from the painted bookshelves, revealing the completed installation with half the room filled with Van Buren's books and modern lamps extending outward.

scenes.heading: EXT. VAN BUREN ESTATE - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Gordon picks up fragments of stained-glass from the driveway beside the removed dome frame when automobile headlights blind him.

scenes.heading: INT. VAN BUREN’S STUDY - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Van Buren Sr. storms into his study in fury, demanding to know who authorized the work, shouting that his mother is frightened outside and a Negro man is roaming the property.
scenes.turningPointNote: Van Buren Sr.'s explosive arrival and racist outburst shatters the completion of László's work, establishing the patriarch's volatile nature and foreshadowing conflict.

scenes.heading: INT. FURNITURE SHOWROOM - BACKROOM - MORNING
scenes.summary: Attila wakes László from exhausted sleep to tell him Harry Lee called and refuses to pay for the work, claiming they damaged the property.

scenes.heading: EXT. OLD CITY CHURCH - MORNING
scenes.summary: The church bell tolls over winter as László's voiceover letter to Erzsébet gives her a new address and asks what she needs.

scenes.heading: INT. OLD CITY CHURCH - SAME
scenes.summary: Homeless men sweep the floor of the Old City Church shelter.

scenes.heading: INT. OLD CITY CHURCH BASEMENT - SAME
scenes.summary: László wakes in a church basement bunk clutching his duffle bag with a grown-out beard and calls to Gordon sleeping across from him.
scenes.turningPointNote: László has fallen to homelessness, marking his complete professional and personal collapse after the Van Buren debacle.

scenes.heading: INT. OLD CITY CHURCH BASEMENT - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Gordon tries to wake his son while a nun approaches asking if László has already left because she wants to speak with him.

scenes.heading: INT. OLD CITY CHURCH BASEMENT LAVATORIES - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Gordon finds László in the bathroom hastily stuffing a syringe and barbiturate powder back in its pouch, and László claims it's for his injury.

scenes.heading: EXT. CONSTRUCTION SITE - MORNING
scenes.summary: László spots Gordon on a construction beam while Gordon explains that Sister Elizabeth wants László to attend Sunday services and collect donations, and László says he already goes somewhere else.

scenes.heading: EXT. CONSTRUCTION SITE - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Van Buren Sr. appears at the construction site overjoyed, telling László he's been looking for him and joking about his beard.
scenes.turningPointNote: Van Buren Sr. seeks out László after their disastrous first encounter, suggesting a reversal that will pull László back into the Van Buren orbit.

scenes.heading: INT. DINER - AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Van Buren shows László a Look Magazine spread featuring László's library design, revealing Van Buren seated in the chaise lounge and standing among the shelving units, with a caption crediting the modern library to the 'forward-thinking man.'

scenes.heading: INT. JAZZ BAR - NIGHT
scenes.summary: László and Gordon attend a raucous jazz set, their features wild and exaggerated through an ultra-bowed lens.

scenes.heading: INT. JAZZ BAR BATHROOM - LATER
scenes.summary: László and Gordon prepare drugs in a jazz bar bathroom while someone pounds on the door, laughing as László pulls out Gordon's antiquated syringe.

scenes.heading: INT. JAZZ BAR - LATER
scenes.summary: László and Gordon, accompanied by strangers, are thrown out by the bar manager after Gordon is caught fingering his girlfriend and László vomits on himself.

scenes.heading: EXT. JAZZ BAR - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: The bar manager and bouncer beat László outside the jazz bar, re-breaking his nose as he laughs madly.

scenes.heading: EXT. OLD CITY CHURCH - MORNING
scenes.summary: László exits a church with his face battered and nose swollen, then notices Van Buren's Town Car parked on the corner with the motor running.

scenes.heading: INT. AUTOMOBILE - LATER
scenes.summary: László sits in the backseat of Van Buren's Town Car trying to pull himself together as the driver informs him there's a pressed shirt and jacket waiting.

scenes.heading: INT. VAN BUREN ESTATE DINING ROOM - LATER
scenes.summary: László arrives at Van Buren's Christmas party in an oversized suit with his smashed face visible, claiming to Van Buren and Harry Lee that he fell off a beam.

scenes.heading: INT. VAN BUREN ESTATE DINING ROOM - LATER
scenes.summary: At the Christmas luncheon, László sits between the Hoffmans while Van Buren brags about his accomplishments, and László reveals his wife is still in Europe, forcibly separated from him.

scenes.heading: INT. FOYER / STUDY - EARLY EVENING
scenes.summary: Van Buren confides in László about his marriage, separation, and his mother Margaret raising him alone after being disowned for having a child out of wedlock, then reveals his grandparents left their fortune to charity out of spite.
scenes.turningPointNote: Van Buren opens up emotionally to László, establishing deeper personal connection and revealing the memorial center's emotional significance

scenes.heading: EXT. FOREST TRAIL/ VISTA - TWILIGHT
scenes.summary: Van Buren leads guests including the Hoffmans up a freezing forest trail to a clearing with a pond, where he announces plans to build a community center in his mother's memory.
scenes.turningPointNote: Van Buren formally proposes the memorial center project to László at the actual site

scenes.heading: INT. VAN BUREN ESTATE FOYER - NIGHT
scenes.summary: László waits alone in the foyer trying to arrange transportation home when Harry Lee enters and tells him his father wants him to stay the night.

scenes.heading: INT. GUEST HOUSE - BEDROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: László wakes in the guest house to find all his belongings have been delivered while he slept, and he quickly checks his bag for his sketchbook as someone knocks at the door.

scenes.heading: INT. VAN BUREN ESTATE - BEDROOM - LATER
scenes.summary: László enters Van Buren's bedroom with sketches in hand to find Van Buren still in bed wearing a sleep mask, hungover and complaining about carpenters causing ringing in his ears.

scenes.heading: INT. HARRY LEE’S PHILADELPHIA OFFICE - DAY
scenes.summary: Harry Lee and Michael Hoffman discuss securing local and state funding by designating a Christian component for the community center, which László questions.

scenes.heading: EXT. COUNTRY ROADS - AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: László rides a bicycle into town while narrating a letter to Erzsébet about an influential American attorney who can help her and Zsófia.

scenes.heading: INT. GUEST HOUSE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: László writes a letter to his wife asking if there's a photograph of her and Zsófia together.

scenes.heading: EXT. DOYLESTOWN - AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: László observes Doylestown from his bicycle, watching schoolchildren, a wedding photo, and teens playing football while narrating his letter asking for anything linking Zsófia to Erzsébet.

scenes.heading: EXT. VISTA - DUSK
scenes.summary: László peacefully sketches the hillside at sunrise while eating a healthful breakfast.

scenes.heading: EXT. VISTA - MORNING
scenes.summary: László walks the landscape's perimeter with a surveyor's wheel while Gordon takes notes, counting measurements aloud as he narrates his letter about good fortune.

scenes.heading: INT. VAN BUREN GUEST HOUSE / DRAWING ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: László works on semi-abstract coal sketches titled 'Margaret Lee Van Buren Center for Activity and Creation,' revealing multiple modular sections of the structure.

scenes.heading: INT. VAN BUREN GUEST HOUSE / DRAWING ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: László works late into the night in his transformed living room, constructing a detailed architectural model of a trapezoidal structure atop the hill overlooking the lake and Doylestown.

scenes.heading: EXT. VAN BUREN ESTATE FOYER - DAY
scenes.summary: László and two servants awkwardly navigate the large-scale model across the estate's horizon line.

scenes.heading: INT. VAN BUREN’S STUDY - LATER
scenes.summary: László presents his finished model to Van Buren, Harry Lee, Maggie Lee, and Leslie Woodrow, explaining the 648 square meter design with convertible multi-functional spaces and a spherical chapel at its heart, while Van Buren gets a haircut and praises the work.
scenes.turningPointNote: László's design is formally presented and approved, moving the project into reality

scenes.heading: INT. GUEST HOUSE - MORNING
scenes.summary: László and Gordon wake up strung out in bed together with a needle still in László's arm, which he rips out causing bleeding as someone bangs on the door.

scenes.heading: EXT. GUEST HOUSE DOOR - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Leslie arrives impatiently at the guest house door, telling László to rinse off and giving him three minutes while he loads the model onto a truck.

scenes.heading: INT. MAYOR KINNEY’S OFFICE - AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: László, Leslie, and Gordon carry the oversized model into Mayor Kinney's office where Harry Lee explains Van Buren is overseas but will telephone tomorrow.

scenes.heading: INT. TOWN HALL - EVENING
scenes.summary: László makes a speech at town hall about the construction creating local jobs, then addresses anonymous written questions probing his background and heritage, defending himself against accusations of being a communist while Van Buren nods encouragingly.

scenes.heading: EXT. LUMBER YARD/ STEEL MANUFACTURER/ MARBLE QUARRY - VARIOUS 79 79
scenes.summary: Materials for the Van Buren Center are prepared around the globe as Erzsébet narrates a letter explaining she obtained the requested items by contacting their upstairs neighbor in Buda.

scenes.heading: EXT. 30TH STREET STATION PLATFORM - MORNING
scenes.summary: László waits nervously at the train platform with the Hoffmans and Maggie Lee, then spots Zsófia and shouts as passengers help lift Erzsébet's wheelchair down from the train.
scenes.turningPointNote: Erzsébet and Zsófia arrive in America, reuniting László with his wife and niece after forced separation

scenes.heading: INT. VAN BUREN ESTATE - DINING ROOM - LATER
scenes.summary: At the Van Buren estate dinner, Van Buren welcomes Erzsébet and Zsófia, praising László's talent and expressing envy of great artists while Erzsébet charms the gathered guests.

scenes.heading: INT. VAN BUREN ESTATE - FOYER - LATER
scenes.summary: As László and Erzsébet prepare to leave, Van Buren stops them to reveal he shared László's plans with another architect for a second opinion, passing him a file with concerns.

scenes.heading: INT. GUEST HOUSE - HALLWAY/ ZSÓFIA’S BEDROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: László shows Zsófia her childishly arranged bedroom, apologizing for remembering her as a little girl, then shows her a framed picture of her beautiful mother, his sister.

scenes.heading: INT. GUEST HOUSE CORRIDOR - LATER
scenes.summary: László struggles to lift Erzsébet into bed in their room as she laughs adoringly.

scenes.heading: INT. GUEST HOUSE BEDROOM - LATER
scenes.summary: Erzsébet and László lie in bed in the dark as she asks if he's angry with her about Van Buren's joke, then questions if he still wants to be with her, to which he replies he loves her.

scenes.heading: INT. GUEST HOUSE - CORRIDOR/ BATHROOM - MORNING
scenes.summary: László opens the bathroom door to find Zsófia helping nude Erzsébet bathe in the tub, and they all flush with embarrassment as he quickly retreats.

scenes.heading: INT. GUEST HOUSE - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: László reviews Van Buren's file in the kitchenette while Erzsébet calls out praising the model, then invites him to visit Attila in the city, but he declines citing an afternoon commitment.

scenes.heading: INT. GUEST HOUSE - BATHROOM - SAME
scenes.summary: Erzsébet tells Zsófia she's missed hearing László mill about in the other room, calling it fantastic.

scenes.heading: INT. VAN BUREN ESTATE - LOUNGE - LATER
scenes.summary: Van Buren watches from his private quarters as Zsófia pushes Erzsébet's wheelchair through the estate's hedge maze, making her smile.

scenes.heading: EXT. VAN BUREN ESTATE - DRIVEWAY - LATER
scenes.summary: Zsófia pushes Erzsébet toward the bus stop as Erzsébet suggests language classes, then Van Buren's town car pulls up beside them.

scenes.heading: INT. AUTOMOBILE - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Van Buren offers to mention Erzsébet to his newspaper friend in New York for a job, and when she hesitates about working there, he suggests she could commute with him at the beginning of each week.

scenes.heading: EXT. VISTA - CONSTRUCTION SITE - TRAILERS - DAY
scenes.summary: László walks fast across the construction site trembling with anger.

scenes.heading: INT. VISTA - CONSTRUCTION SITE - OFFICE TRAILER - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: László bursts into the office trailer and throws a file at Leslie's head, accusing him of going behind his back to have Van Buren meet with another designer, James T. Simpson.

scenes.heading: EXT. VISTA - CONSTRUCTION SITE - TRENCHES - MORNING
scenes.summary: In pouring rain, László leads Van Buren, Harry Lee, Leslie, Mayor Kinney, Michael Hoffman, and Jim Simpson through the foundation trenches explaining the construction, but Simpson interrupts aggressively questioning the design and calling it a 'goddamned monstrosity.'

scenes.heading: EXT. VISTA - CONSTRUCTION SITE - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Mayor Kinney expresses doubts to Van Buren about László and the design, noting daily complaints, but Van Buren promises to do a community event and confirms Jim will stay on.

scenes.heading: EXT. VISTA - AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: At a ribbon cutting ceremony, Van Buren, Harry Lee, László, and Mayor Kinney pose for photographs as Erzsébet whispers to László in Hungarian that she's proud of him and wants him to make love to her tonight.

scenes.heading: EXT. VISTA - WATERING HOLE - DUSK
scenes.summary: At sunset by the watering hole, Zsófia bathes as Harry Lee swims up to greet her, while nearby Erzsébet, László, Van Buren, and Maggie Lee laugh together joyously on the shore.

scenes.heading: EXT. VISTA - LATER
scenes.summary: Zsófia and Harry Lee emerge from the water to join Van Buren, Maggie Lee, and Erzsébet while László talks with Gordon and his son William about the construction crew sleeping on-site.

scenes.heading: EXT. VISTA - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: László tells Van Buren they must leave to start work early tomorrow, then wheels Erzsébet away as she questions the rush and he matter-of-factly states he's forming a crew.

scenes.heading: INT. GUEST HOUSE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Erzsébet hosts a peasant's supper for László, Zsófia, Gordon, and William to celebrate their hard work, and Gordon reveals his wife Augusta died of a tooth infection in 1943.

scenes.heading: INT. GUEST HOUSE - LIVING ROOM - LATER
scenes.summary: Erzsébet reviews László's drawings while he enters and asks her opinion; she notes the unusual design with small rooms and high ceilings, and begins to ask which part they are paying for.

scenes.heading: EXT. VISTA - DAY
scenes.summary: László and Gordon supervise crew members laying concrete foundation at the transformed construction site.

scenes.heading: INT. THE CONGREGATION MIKVEH ISRAEL - MIKVEH
scenes.summary: The Toth family and the Hoffmans arrive at a basement mikveh for overflow Yom Kippur service; Erzsébet learns the community is growing.

scenes.heading: INT. THE CONGREGATION MIKVEH ISRAEL - LATER
scenes.summary: László and Michael Hoffman recite the Viddui prayer, rhythmically pounding their chests; László hits his chest with considerable force.

scenes.heading: EXT. TRAIN DEPOT - MORNING
scenes.summary: Crew members carefully load monolithic concrete slabs onto a flatbed train car.

scenes.heading: EXT. MEADOW - RAILROAD - EVENING
scenes.summary: A train carrying the concrete slabs derails with catastrophic explosions, viewed from an ultra-wide distance that makes the disaster appear small against the landscape.
scenes.turningPointNote: The train derailment destroys the materials and threatens the entire project, marking a major crisis point.

scenes.heading: INT. GUEST HOUSE BEDROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Erzsébet wakes screaming in pain and sends László to get Zsófia and her medication while she writhes in the sheets.

scenes.heading: INT. VISTA - CONSTRUCTION SITE - OFFICE TRAILER - DAY
scenes.summary: Leslie Woodrow receives news by phone that a section came undone taking seven freight cars with it; Van Buren erupts in anger learning their own transport company was responsible, then fires everyone including László.
scenes.turningPointNote: László is fired from the project after the derailment, seemingly ending his dream commission.

scenes.heading: EXT. VISTA - CONSTRUCTION SITE - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: László apologizes to Gordon and offers him money while they're both let go; László asks if Gordon has any hop and Gordon says he's off it.

scenes.heading: INT. GUEST HOUSE - LATER
scenes.summary: László smashes his model in fury while Erzsébet tells him to stop acting like a child; he declares it's over but she insists he go re-engage Van Buren, and he tells her two people are hospitalized.

scenes.heading: EXT. NEW YORK CITY’S MIDTOWN EAST (MURRAY HILL) - DAY
scenes.summary: The view shows the East River towards the United Nations and New York City skyline.

scenes.heading: INT. MIDTOWN EAST STREET LOBBY - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Michael Hoffman enters the lobby of an office building.

scenes.heading: INT. RUDOLPH HEYWOOD & ASSOCIATES LOBBY - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Michael Hoffman asks the receptionist for László Toth and is directed upstairs to the draftsmen.

scenes.heading: INT. RUDOLPH HEYWOOD & ASSOCIATES DRAWING ROOM - MOMENTS 114 114
scenes.summary: Michael Hoffman walks through a drawing room of thirty draftsmen to find an aged and decayed László smoking at a drawing board with visible track marks on his arms.

scenes.heading: INT. LÁSZLÓ AND ERZSÉBET’S TENEMENT - KITCHEN - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Zsófia announces she and Binyamin are making Aliyah to Jerusalem; Erzsébet is heartbroken and László questions the decision, but Zsófia insists it's their obligation and declares she is Jewish, her child will be Jewish, and she will not raise her child in America.
scenes.turningPointNote: Zsófia's decision to leave for Israel removes Erzsébet's support system and deepens the family's fracture.

scenes.heading: INT. TENEMENT BUILDING - BEDROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Erzsébet holds László and makes him promise not to let Zsófia's departure drive him mad.

scenes.heading: INT. TENEMENT BUILDING - BATHROOM - MORNING
scenes.summary: László dismantles a syringe in his toiletry pouch to make it less recognizable and tucks it into his luggage before Erzsébet calls that it's time to go.

scenes.heading: EXT. CARRARA TOWN SQUARE - CAFE - DAY
scenes.summary: László and Van Buren sip coffee at an outdoor cafe in Carrara; Van Buren comments that László looks a mess and complains about waiting for someone, criticizing Italians for being late.

scenes.heading: EXT. CARRARA MARBLE QUARRY - LATER
scenes.summary: Van Buren, Orazio, and László walk across the massive Carrara marble quarry surrounded by colossal blocks of stone; Van Buren complains about wearing the wrong shoes.

scenes.heading: INT. ORAZIO’S ATELIER - NIGHT
scenes.summary: A group of artisans and masons drink and dance together in Orazio's atelier surrounded by sculptures, singing along to music.

scenes.heading: INT. ORAZIO’S ATELIER - LATER
scenes.summary: Van Buren searches for László and finds him slouched against a marble column with an unspooled pouch of hop gear in his lap, eyes rolled back and barely responsive; Van Buren slides down beside him.

scenes.heading: EXT. CARRARA MARBLE QUARRY - DAY
scenes.summary: Van Buren walks ahead of László up the quarry trail in searing white heat, calling out about László's state the previous night and humming while László stumbles behind him.

scenes.heading: INT. TENEMENT BUILDING - KITCHEN - DAY
scenes.summary: Erzsébet composes a letter to Zsófia congratulating her on her baby and mentioning enclosed banknotes from László.

scenes.heading: EXT. VISTA - CONSTRUCTION SITE - DAY
scenes.summary: The construction site is active again in ultra-fast motion showing cargo unloading, cement mixing, scaffolding erected, and various construction activities over time.

scenes.heading: INT. INSTITUTE STAIRWELL - CONSTRUCTION SITE - DUSK
scenes.summary: László and Jim Simpson argue feverishly while marching up a staircase through the construction site, their dialogue muted while Erzsébet's voiceover describes her loneliness and László closing a door to her.

scenes.heading: EXT. VISTA - CONSTRUCTION SITE - DUSK
scenes.summary: László walks with a lamp inspecting freshly laid concrete for the cistern, then ascends to find an exposed staircase leading to nowhere while Erzsébet's voiceover notes he no longer attends synagogue.

scenes.heading: EXT. VISTA - CONSTRUCTION SITE - DAY
scenes.summary: László explains to employees about leaving four-inch gaps between flats for sunlight; when the construction supervisor questions this unplanned change, László insists on keeping it and says he'll think about inserting glass sheets, then abruptly fires the supervisor when challenged.

scenes.heading: INT. AUTOMOBILE - EVENING
scenes.summary: In the car, Erzsébet criticizes how László treated a boy and Gordon; László insists their safety is his priority and declares he'll finish this or they are finished, prompting Erzsébet to call him a selfish old bastard and say living with him is impossible.

scenes.heading: INT. TENEMENT BUILDING - BEDROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Erzsébet cries out in pain from a recurring night terror; László tries to calm her but she insists she's in pain and close to death, demanding her pills.

scenes.heading: INT. TENEMENT BUILDING - BATHROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: László frantically rifles through the medicine cabinet while Erzsébet wails in the other room, finding only half a pill in an otherwise empty bottle.

scenes.heading: INT. TENEMENT BUILDING - BEDROOM - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: László tells Erzsébet there's only half a pill and asks if she has more in her purse; she shakes her head and weeps that the pain won't stop.

scenes.heading: INT. TENEMENT BUILDING - BATHROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: László pulls down his toiletry pouch from the medicine cabinet and retrieves a spoon and dropper.

scenes.heading: INT. TENEMENT BUILDING - BEDROOM - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: László injects Erzsébet with heroin, telling her it's what they gave him on the boat for his broken face; after she calms, he prepares and administers a dose to himself.
scenes.turningPointNote: László gives Erzsébet heroin for her pain and then uses himself, crossing a devastating threshold that will destroy them both.

scenes.heading: INT. TENEMENT BUILDING - BEDROOM - MORNING
scenes.summary: László lies in Erzsébet's arms in beautiful morning light and she whispers asking if he has more.

scenes.heading: INT. TENEMENT BUILDING - KITCHEN - LATER
scenes.summary: László closes the blinds to keep out the harsh afternoon sunlight.

scenes.heading: INT. TENEMENT BUILDING - BEDROOM - LATER
scenes.summary: László and Erzsébet make love intensely in the darkened room while he tells her he loves her in Hungarian.

scenes.heading: INT. TENEMENT BUILDING - BEDROOM - LATER
scenes.summary: Erzsébet moans that she needs the toilet and László begins to scoop her in his arms.

scenes.heading: INT. TENEMENT BUILDING - BATHROOM - CONTINUOUS 138 138
scenes.summary: László finishes placing Erzsébet on the toilet seat and tells her he'll wait outside.

scenes.heading: INT. TENEMENT BUILDING - BEDROOM - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: László calls to Erzsébet asking if she's finished, receives no response, knocks, then opens the door to find her foaming at the mouth.
scenes.turningPointNote: Erzsébet overdoses, a direct consequence of László's decision to give her heroin, bringing catastrophic consequences.

scenes.heading: EXT. MANHATTAN HOSPITAL - NIGHT
scenes.summary: László runs carrying Erzsébet to the hospital shouting for help; a nurse assures him she's breathing but he insists she's dying and apologizes in Hungarian.

scenes.heading: EXT. VISTA - CONSTRUCTION SITE - DAWN
scenes.summary: The marble altarpiece is unwrapped at the construction site while Erzsébet's voice describes meeting God and calling him by name, then asks László what he did to her; he responds that it was an accident.

scenes.heading: INT. HOSPITAL ROOM - DAWN
scenes.summary: In the hospital, Erzsébet asks if László remembers his confession; she tells him the country is rotten and announces she's going to Israel to be with Zsófia and her granddaughter.
scenes.turningPointNote: Erzsébet decides to leave László and America for Israel, marking the dissolution of their marriage.

scenes.heading: EXT. VAN BUREN ESTATE - GATES - EVENING
scenes.summary: Erzsébet arrives by taxi at the closed Van Buren estate gates and her driver helps her arrange herself with a walker.

scenes.heading: EXT. VAN BUREN ESTATE - DRIVEWAY - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Erzsébet makes her way up the driveway to the front door with her walker, breathing hard.

scenes.heading: INT. VAN BUREN ESTATE - FOYER - LATER
scenes.summary: Maggie Lee greets Erzsébet in the foyer and asks if László is with her; Erzsébet says just her and asks if Van Buren is in, learning they're sitting down to dinner.

scenes.heading: INT. VAN BUREN ESTATE - DINING ROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Erzsébet enters the dining room where Van Buren, Harry Lee, and associates are seated; she refuses to sit and tells them she has something very difficult to say, then accuses Van Buren of raping her husband in Italy.
scenes.turningPointNote: Erzsébet publicly accuses Van Buren of raping László, a devastating revelation that cannot be walked back and shatters all relationships.

scenes.heading: INT. VAN BUREN ESTATE - FOYER - LATER
scenes.summary: Harry Lee drags Erzsébet to the front door where she falls; Maggie Lee screams and comes to help while Erzsébet shouts shame at them; Harry Lee tells her never to come back and sets her walker outside.

scenes.heading: INT. VAN BUREN ESTATE - DINING ROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Harry Lee apologizes to the guests for the interruption and asks them not to leave yet, but they depart anyway noting Van Buren has gone to bed.

scenes.heading: INT. VAN BUREN ESTATE - FOYER - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Harry Lee walks back through the foyer shouting to his father that it's over and she's gone, then turns a corner.

scenes.heading: INT. VAN BUREN ESTATE - STAIRWELL - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Harry Lee moves up the stairwell.

scenes.heading: INT. VAN BUREN ESTATE - CORRIDOR - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Harry Lee searches his father's bedroom and study, finding both empty, then frantically opens every door on the floor while shouting for his father and instructing Maggie Lee to call for him outside.

scenes.heading: INT. VAN BUREN ESTATE - STAIRWELL - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Harry Lee descends the stairs with increasing panic.

scenes.heading: INT. VAN BUREN ESTATE - FOYER - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Harry Lee crosses the foyer and exits through the front door to the driveway.

scenes.heading: EXT. VAN BUREN ESTATE - DRIVEWAY
scenes.summary: Harry Lee desperately shouts for his father outside while Maggie Lee also calls out asking if he can hear them.

scenes.heading: EXT. VAN BUREN ESTATE - FOREST - DAWN
scenes.summary: A search team of local volunteers spread ten meters apart combs a snowy forest at dawn, calling out Harrison's name.

scenes.heading: EXT. VISTA - CONSTRUCTION SITE - DUSK
scenes.summary: Volunteers move across the construction site of László's institute design, revealing its austere beauty through the modern ruins.

scenes.heading: EXT. INSTITUTE CORRIDORS - SAME
scenes.summary: The search party uses flashlights to investigate the institute corridors.

scenes.heading: INT. CISTERN - SAME
scenes.summary: Volunteers walk the perimeter of the rainwater harvesting system below grade, calling out Harrison's name.

scenes.heading: INT. CHAPEL - SAME
scenes.summary: A volunteer walks past the marble altarpiece in the chapel as a voice announces they've found something, while sunlight forms a cross and snow falls from above through an opening.
scenes.turningPointNote: Van Buren is discovered (implied by 'we've got something'), marking the resolution of his disappearance and transition to the epilogue set decades later in 1980 Venice.

scenes.heading: EXT. VENICE, ITALY - EVENING
scenes.summary: A series of angles establish the city of Venice, Italy in the evening in 1980 at the First Architecture Biennale.

scenes.heading: INT. GIARDINI - ARCHITECTURE BIENNALE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: A lavish opening night gala is underway with a small crowd gathering around the Israeli Pavilion at the Giardini.

scenes.heading: INT. CENTRAL PAVILION - MAIN GALLERY - SAME
scenes.summary: Zsófia pushes an elderly, frail László in a wheelchair through the main gallery past rows of ornate architectural models, commenting that they look beautiful, and László nods, too frail to speak.

scenes.heading: INT. CENTRAL PAVILION - SOUTH GALLERY - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: László raises his hand to stop Zsófia from leaving as he observes a projection of a macro-tour of his model work on the gallery wall, while Zsófia tells him the director wants to speak before the ceremony.

scenes.heading: INT. ISRAELI PAVILION - LATER
scenes.summary: Zsófia delivers a tearful speech about her uncle László's principled artistry, his concept of 'Hard Core of Beauty,' and his philosophy of directing perception to the world as it is, beginning to recount his birth in 1911 in Austria-Hungary.
scenes.turningPointNote: The final scene provides closure to László's arc, revealing his survival, recognition, and legacy through his niece's tribute at the Venice Biennale, completing the film's meditation on artistic integrity and endurance.
